title: viola × pseudomakinoi
intro: Viola × pseudomakinoi, also known as the hybrid Japanese violet, is a rare and endangered plant species native to Japan, resulting from the cross between Viola mandshurica and Viola philippica. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Viola × pseudomakinoi include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activities. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is prepared in various ways, including infusion, decoction, and tincture, although further research is needed to confirm its safety and efficacy as a medicinal agent.
